context: Tests whether the agent correctly uses Magento 2 plugins over class rewrites, chooses appropriate plugin types (preferring before/after over around), registers the plugin in the correct frontend scope, implements observers with try/catch to avoid blocking critical flows, and documents the design rationale.

checklist.name: Plugin class, not preference
checklist.description: The name modification is implemented as a Plugin class (with before/after/aroundGetName or similar method), NOT as a <preference> rewrite of the Product model

checklist.name: No around plugin for name
checklist.description: The plugin for modifying the product name does NOT use an around method (aroundGetName) — uses afterGetName or beforeGetName instead

checklist.name: Plugin registered in frontend scope
checklist.description: The product name plugin is registered in etc/frontend/di.xml (not in etc/di.xml global scope)

checklist.name: Plugin registered with type and name
checklist.description: etc/frontend/di.xml contains a <type> element with a <plugin> child that has name, type (class), and sortOrder attributes

checklist.name: Observer implements ObserverInterface
checklist.description: The observer class implements Magento\Framework\Event\ObserverInterface and has an execute(Observer $observer): void method

checklist.name: Observer has try/catch
checklist.description: The observer's execute() method wraps its logic in a try/catch block and does NOT re-throw or let exceptions propagate

checklist.name: Observer logs error, not throws
checklist.description: In the catch block of the observer, the exception is logged (e.g., via LoggerInterface) rather than thrown or silently swallowed without any action

checklist.name: Observer registered in events.xml
checklist.description: etc/events.xml (or etc/frontend/events.xml) exists and contains an <event> element with an <observer> child referencing the observer class

checklist.name: declare(strict_types=1) in PHP files
checklist.description: Every PHP file in the module begins with declare(strict_types=1) after <?php

checklist.name: No Object Manager direct usage
checklist.description: No PHP file contains ObjectManager::getInstance() — all dependencies injected via constructor

checklist.name: Design notes: plugin preference
checklist.description: design-notes.md explains that plugins are composable and avoid conflicts compared to preferences/rewrites

checklist.name: Design notes: plugin type choice
checklist.description: design-notes.md identifies the chosen plugin type (before/after) and explicitly states that around plugins are avoided or used sparingly because they can prevent other plugins from running
